title: Warehouse Assistant Manager
description:
Job Overview

We are seeking a highly competent Warehouse Assistant Manager to oversee full warehouse activities and ensure seamless 3PL operations. The ideal candidate must be bilingual in Japanese and English to effectively communicate and collaborate with Japanese-speaking associates and stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities

1.    Plan, organize and control all warehouse operations

2.    Develop standard operating procedures and other operational guidelines.

3.    Planning cost saving solution and continuous improvement for operation productivity.

4.    Daily liaison with customers on safety, quality and productivity requirements; attend related meetings, discussions and site patrols.

5.    Optimize the layout and storage systems for easy access to goods and safe movement of employees and equipment.

6.    Oversee document recording and system data entry.

7.    Oversee the preparation and timely dispatch of outgoing orders, ensuring proper packaging, labeling, and shipment tracking. Monitor and track the status of shipments to ensure orders are completed on time.

8.     Investigate and resolve customer complaints and non-conformities

9.     Participate in KPI review meetings with clients

10.   To provide ongoing guidance on warehouse procedures and equipment operation. Monitor staff performance and ensure productivity targets are met.

11.  To conduct manpower planning and deployment for smooth workflow.

12.   To manage staff training and development initiatives.

Requirements

1.    Minimum 4 Years’ Experience in 3PL Operations

2.    Preferably working experiences specializing in Warehouse & Logistic Management

3.    Experience in warehouse operations and space utilization planning

4.    Excellent organizational, planning, people management and problem-solving skills

5.   Strong proficiency in manpower allocation planning and staff training

6. Preferably with knowledge of industry certifications including TAPA, GMP, GDP and Halal

7.    Experience in conducting risk assessments

8.    Familiarity with implementing innovative technologies in warehouse operations

9.    You must be bilingual in Japanese and English (speaking, reading and writing proficiency required) to effectively communicate and collaborate with Japanese-speaking associates and stakeholders.

postedCompany.description:
SANKYU (SINGAPORE) PTE LTD

We are a well-established multinational Company with diversified local and regional dealings in the Logistics and Engineering Industry.

We have decades of experience in providing cost-effective and integrated solutions to our customers.

Our main philosophies are: Quality, Safety, Health, Environment and Business Continuity at all times to our customers.

We believe that it’s our people who make our company what it is, we have a team of great people, with a shared mission, vision and values.


Sankyu sees the value of social responsibility and is committed to sustainable procurement, which includes the highest standards of social and environmental responsibility and ethical conduct through:- Labour and Human Rights, Ethics, Privacy & Information Security, Compliance, Safety, Health & Environment.


Sankyu provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) in all our employment practices to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, genetic information, marital status, military status, or any other category protected by federal, state, or local laws.  Sankyu's EEO practices are upheld in every location that it operates and in all aspects of the employment relationship, including hiring, recruiting, placement, transfer, promotion, compensation, discipline, termination, layoff, recall, training, and leaves of absence.


You are encouraged to visit our website at http://www.sankyu.com.sg for detailed information about our Company and more career opportunities.
